item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Una, Nijimura, Abdul,Brown, and Pendleton? I see what you did there…

Silly references aside, love the scip. Nice work!

item.title: Novote
item.content:encoded: The article, while I can clearly tell plenty of effort was put in, is not quite good enough to convince me to upvote it. Notably, the dialogue sounds… off. A lot of it just sounds unnatural, not at all what someone would say in that situation. Especially during the O5 meeting. There are some interesting ideas here, but it all feels scattered and not executed well enough. If I can give a personal recommendation based on my opinion here, I'd say work on making dialogue sound natural and try to write with a more focused narrative in mind.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Made a tiny correction.

Finding an entrance back into the tunnel took quite a bit of time I went down the ally way which I came

Changed ally way into alleyway.Hope that's right.

item.title: Re: +1
item.content:encoded: Ah good eye. Thanks for fixing it!

great scip i absolutley loved every part of it

Silicene was held for several seconds as O5-9 communicated with his interpreter.

minor spelling error i changed "Silicene" to silence if this was not an error please tell me

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It comes to a satisfying conclusion, but I must admit a certain amount of skip fatigue by the end. The first exploration log is interesting, but long; the second gives us more info about the labyrinth, but I never felt like any of it was really important, and it is also long. Paring might be in order.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
the first half of the article is very interesting, with a compelling exploration log.

as soon as we get to the journal — and thus, the second half of the article — it feels like all the momentum is lost. the anomaly itself is very interesting, but it doesn't feel like the second half does enough to explain / demystify the anomaly (or tries to leave too much to mystery).

overall, it's a novote from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The O5 meeting deserves a really good proofread, I understand it's the end of a very long article but there were maybe a good dozen obvious errors, mostly grammatical, a few misused or skipped words. The rest was much better on that front so I'm guessing it's a simple matter of being tired or in a hurry towards the end.

item.title: Re: Junior Staff - Site Crit
item.content:encoded: Thank you for this feedback. There's always room to improve as an author, and we will be sure to take this into mind when considering how to potentially improve this article in the future.

item.title: Junior Staff - Site Crit
item.content:encoded:
[+ Opinion]
– hide block

The exploration log of this relies on extremely forced characterisation. We don't learn about the individuals organically, we're told exactly how they act in a log that seems extraneous and unrealistic from an in-universe perspective. The exploration also relies on extremely convenient corruptions to avoid having to create narrative escalation itself, and the journal log does a similar trick while being entirely unmemorable.

The poem seems unnecessary and only tacked on to fit a poem in to the article. I'm also not a fan of its stilted form and overuse of periods. The big reveal was also fairly disappointing; just having this be less contained because it somehow makes more anomalies both tries to suggest all anomalies are connected in some way (which I generally don't like, and this doesn't have the buildup or lore to support an implication like this) and doesn't have any real emotional impact on me as a reader. Being given an unfinished O5 Log also felt immensely disappointing, and a similar cheap method of withholding information as extraneous redactions.

Overall, there just wasn't much in this article to be attached to, as any interesting narrative escalation was obfuscated with blackboxes, convenient file corruption etc.

Disclaimer: Opinions expressed in this review do not reflect the opinions of SCP Wiki staff as a whole. Please respond to gee0765 or contact the captain of the Site Criticism Team, SoullessSingularity, for any questions or concerns relating to this review.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Doot. This was a blast. pr0m37h3um was great to work with. 9/8 would do this again.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
"All that is gold does not glitter,

Not all those who wander are lost;

The old that is strong does not wither,

Deep roots are not reached by the frost."

- J. R. R. Tolkien

So this is a big one. Hopefully we did a good.

Thank you so much to my wonderful co-author, Doctor North. I don't think either of us could have done this without each other and it was a great time working with you.

Thanks to Researcher Sparrow, Dr Raveren, and others for critiquing this.

And thank you for taking the time to read and vote on this.

In case we do not win, here are our top 5 slots other than 5000:
1. 5005
2. 5050
3. 5999
4. 5500
5. 5995

Read More from These Authors:

pr0m37h3um's Author Page
North's Author Page
